channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Parents of American murdered by Hamas make 'plea' to Trump after latest hostage release
item.description: The parents of murdered Israeli-American hostage Hersh Goldberg-Polin pleaded with President Donald Trump to get the remaining 76 hostages home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sam Nujoma, Namibia's fiery freedom fighter and first president, dies aged 95
item.description: OSHAKATI, Namibia (AP) — Sam Nujoma, the fiery, white-bearded freedom fighter who led Namibia to independence from apartheid South Africa in 1990 and served as its first president for 15 years, coming to be known as the father of his nation, has died. He was 95.
item.dc:creator: ELIASER NDEYANALE, FARAI MUTSAKA and GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 7.6 magnitude earthquake shakes the Caribbean, some islands urge residents to leave coastlines
item.description: A magnitude-7.6 earthquake shook the Caribbean Sea south of the Cayman Islands Saturday, according to the U.S. Geological Survey. Several islands and countries urged people near the coastline to move inland but authorities in most places later lifted the tsunami alerts.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's AI ambition, China's DeepSeek overshadow an AI summit
item.description: U.S. Vice President JD Vance — making his first trip abroad since taking office — will attend the Paris AI Action Summit starting Feb. 10.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIE CORBET and KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Ecuador votes for president, with the conservative incumbent and a leftist lawyer in the lead
item.description: Polls have opened in a presidential election in Ecuador that has shaped up to be a repeat of the 2023 race, when voters chose a young, conservative millionaire over the leftist protégée of the country’s most influential president this century.
item.dc:creator: Regina Garcia Cano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dalai Lama's elder brother, who led several rounds of talks with China, dies at 97
item.description: The elder brother of the Dalai Lama and former chairman of the Tibetan government-in-exile in India, Gyalo Thondup, who led several rounds of talks with China and worked with foreign governments for the Tibetan cause, has died.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Released Thai hostages return to Bangkok after being held for over a year in Gaza
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Five Thai workers released after being held hostage for over a year in Gaza arrived in Bangkok on Sunday. Sarusak Rumnao, 32, Watchara Sriaoun, 33, Sathian Suwannakham, 35, Pongsak Thaenna, 36, and Bannawat Saethao, 27, were freed on Jan. 30 as part of an exchange arrangement.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Hamas releases 3 frail-looking Israeli hostages for Palestinian prisoners under Gaza ceasefire
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Hamas-led militants released three gaunt, frail-looking Israeli hostages and Israel freed nearly 200 Palestinian prisoners Saturday in the latest exchange of a ceasefire that has paused 16 months of war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, MOHAMMAD JAHJOUH and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Mother, son die from food poisoning at Dominican Republic resort
item.description: A grieving family filed a lawsuit after a mother and son died from food poisoning while vacationing at a Dominican Republic resort.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: War-torn Lebanon forms its first government in over 2 years to end deadlock
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s new prime minister on Saturday formed the country’s first full-fledged government since 2022.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump halts aid to South Africa citing new land law
item.description: President Trump signed an executive order formally halting all aid to South Africa and urged his Cabinet to come up with a resettlement program for Afrikaners who he claims "are victims of unjust racial discrimination,” citing the country’s new land law intended to combat the racist apartheid era.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: A Russian spy ship caught fire off Syria's coast, officials say. Here's audio of its broadcasts
item.description: PARIS (AP) — The man is insistent: Our ship is in difficulty, so keep your distance, he instructs another vessel over the radio. “Warship on your course,” he says. "I am drifting. I’m not under command.
item.dc:creator: JOHN LEICESTER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Netanyahu: 'No future' in Gaza unless 'Hamas destroyed' amid concern for hostages
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu said Hamas must be 'destroyed,' following discussions with President Trump and lawmakers on the Hill, despite concerns that hard rhetoric could jeopardize hostage releases.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine's Western backers will meet for arms talks as doubts over US intentions grow
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — The main international forum for drumming up weapons and ammunition for Ukraine will for the first time meet under the auspices of a country other than the U.S. as uncertainty surrounds the future of Washington’s support for arming the war-torn country.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: São Paulo, Brazil small plane crash kills 2
item.description: A small plane crashed into a bus on a road in São Paulo, Brazil, on Friday, killing the pilot and co-pilot and injuring two people on the ground, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia claims it's taken another eastern Ukraine town. Kyiv officials make no comment
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia’s Defense Ministry claimed on Friday that its forces had captured the coal mining town of Toretsk in their latest breakthrough in eastern Ukraine’s Donetsk region, where Ukrainian defenses are creaking.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and BARRY HATTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ICC condemns sanctions by Trump, pledges to continue its work
item.description: The International Criminal Court said the move was an attempt to “harm its independent and impartial judicial work.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Panama's president denies making a deal that US warships can transit the canal for free
item.description: PANAMA CITY (AP) — Panamanian President José Raúl Mulino on Thursday denied the U.S. State Department’s claim that his country had reached a deal allowing U.S. warships to transit the Panama Canal for free. Mulino said he had told U.S.
item.dc:creator: ALMA SOLÍS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump’s Gaza Strip proposal reverberates
item.description: Aftershocks reverberated Wednesday from President Trump’s declaration the previous day that the United States should take “long-term ownership” of Gaza and that the Palestinians who live there should be moved.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, WGN 9

item.title: U.S. allies, adversaries reject Trump suggestion to 'take over' Gaza Strip
item.description: Both U.S. allies and adversaries alike rejected President Donald Trump’s suggestion that the U.S. “take over” the war-torn Gaza Strip and turn it into the “Riviera of the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: USPS lifts suspension of Hong Kong and China parcels
item.description: The U.S. Postal Service reversed course Wednesday, saying it would continue to accept all inbound mail and packages from China and Hong Kong.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Trump's idea to clear Gaza is a real estate grab: Rep. Auchincloss
item.description: President Trump and his son-in-law view the Gaza Strip as “developable property," a Democratic lawmaker says.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Trump floats US taking over Gaza Strip
item.description: President Trump on Tuesday floated the idea of the U.S. taking over the Gaza Strip, saying America should be responsible for clearing the territory of rubble and unexploded bombs and proposing an “economic development.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9
